title: Coastal scrub identity and threshold support phase
summary: Lane 2 should make Coastal Scrub feel more like a destination, then reinforce treeline and tundra as memorable exposure-threshold spaces through compact authored support.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the coastal scrub identity support handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Coastal Scrub still needs stronger emotional distinctiveness.

execution_order.goal: Implement the coastal scrub identity support pack.
execution_order.why_now: Forest should not remain the only biome with strong remembered support surfaces.

execution_order.goal: Review the coastal scrub identity support pack.
execution_order.why_now: The biome should feel more destination-like without growing verbose.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the threshold-support handoff for treeline and tundra.
execution_order.why_now: The second act should lean harder into exposure, threshold, and short-season texture.

execution_order.goal: Implement the treeline and tundra threshold support pack.
execution_order.why_now: The high country should teach shelter loss and harshness through authored support, not just route text.
The second act should feel more memorable without piling on prose.

guardrails: Do not solve identity by extending route-board copy.
Prefer note-backed, visual-first, close-look, comparison, and sketchbook support.

themes: woody cover overtaking open dune bloom
scrub as a place, not just an in-between

treeline_focus.teaching_goal: show the last tree-shaped shelter bending into open fell
treeline_focus.suggested_sentence: Cold wind keeps this spruce low and bent instead of tall.

tundra_focus.teaching_goal: give short-season tundra one compact remembered bloom clue
tundra_focus.suggested_note: Fuzzy bloom catching one brief thaw on open tundra.
